title: Cloudflare Edge WAF & Bot Protection — managed rulesets, rate-limit baseline, plan-aware bot intent
description: A platform engineer fronts a public application with Cloudflare WAF and bot controls using Hulumi's Edge Platform (v1.3.2). Target end state: EdgeWafBaseline applies the Cloudflare-managed and OWASP managed rulesets, a small set of typed custom rules, and a login/auth/API rate-limit baseline; BotProtectionBaseline maps a bounded intent (simple, balanced, granular) to the controls the account plan can actually deliver, and surfaces unsupported/degraded controls instead of silently downgrading.
actors: Misconfigured User (semi-trusted; may request an Enterprise-only control on a lower plan)
External Attacker (untrusted; exploit traffic, scraping, credential stuffing)
assets: WAF managed and custom rule configuration
Bot-intent selection and the plan it maps to
The public application behind the edge

stride.name: Exploit traffic reaches origin without managed WAF
stride.description: A raw zone with no managed ruleset lets injection/exploit traffic reach the origin application. Mitigation: EdgeWafBaseline applies the Cloudflare-managed and OWASP managed rulesets plus typed custom rules so common exploit classes are filtered at the edge.

stride.name: Volumetric / credential-stuffing on unprotected endpoints
stride.description: Login, auth, and API endpoints without rate limiting are exposed to volumetric abuse and credential stuffing. Mitigation: EdgeWafBaseline ships a login/auth/API rate-limit baseline with explicit bounds rather than unbounded generated rules.

stride.name: Bots impersonate legitimate users
stride.description: Scrapers and credential-stuffing bots impersonate real users against the application. Mitigation: BotProtectionBaseline normalizes a bounded intent enum to the strongest bot control the plan supports (Bot Fight Mode / Super Bot Fight Mode / Enterprise Bot Management).

stride.name: False security from silent plan degradation (overtrust)
stride.description: A user selects an Enterprise-only control (granular bot management, account-wide ruleset) on a plan that cannot deliver it. If the component silently degraded, the user would believe a control exists that Cloudflare never deployed. Mitigation: plan-aware mapping plus unsupportedControls / degradedControls outputs and test snapshots make the gap explicit.

stride.name: Raw ruleset resource bypasses typed inputs
stride.description: A raw Cloudflare ruleset resource beside Hulumi components bypasses EdgeWafBaseline's typed rule inputs and bounded rate-limit design. Mitigation: prefer the component; the Cloudflare hardening pack flags raw unsafe edge configuration at preview time.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.EdgeWafBaseline
recommendedComponents.rationale: Managed + OWASP rulesets, bounded typed custom rules, and a login/auth/API rate-limit baseline, with a plan-aware unsupported-controls output instead of silent downgrade.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/cloudflare-baseline.BotProtectionBaseline
recommendedComponents.rationale: Maps a bounded intent enum (simple/balanced/granular) to the strongest bot control the account plan supports and lists degraded controls so users do not overtrust a lower plan.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.cloudflare.HulumiCloudflareHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (Edge Platform; rule IDs stable)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Preview-time guardrails over raw Cloudflare edge configuration that would bypass the typed WAF/bot baselines.
